column5: The New School Revolution (NSR) is a movement within tabletop role-playing games (TTRPGs) that emphasizes innovative design, streamlined mechanics, and unique settings, moving away from traditional fantasy tropes. NSR games often feature rules-light systems, emergent narratives, and a focus on exploration and creativity, offering players fresh and unconventional gaming experiences.

column4: Game systems where characters choose specific classes or archetypes, each with unique abilities and progression paths.

Rules‑light games keep mechanics minimal so you can get to the table fast and stay in the story.
column5: Rules‑light (aka rules‑lite) TTRPGs cut bookkeeping and edge cases so you can play sooner and focus on fiction. Expect: streamlined character creation, simple resolution (often 1–2 dice), and minimal modifiers. Great for one‑shots, new players, and tables that prefer rulings over rules. They’re not just ‘simple’—many are razor‑sharp by design (OSR/NSR titles, narrative engines, and modern indie systems). Browse below for quick starts and deeper picks across fantasy, horror, sci‑fi, and more.

Games that are compatible with or based on Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition mechanics. These systems use the d20 core, similar class structures, and can often use 5e modules and supplements with minimal conversion.
column5:
What is 5e Compatible?

"5e Compatible" games are tabletop RPGs that use the core mechanics of Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition (D&D 5e) or are designed to work seamlessly with 5e content. This includes:

  • Direct compatibility – Games that use the same d20 system, proficiency bonuses, and core mechanics as 5e
  • 5e-based systems – Games that build on 5e foundations with additional rules or modifications
  • OSR/retro-clones – Old-School Renaissance games that share DNA with early D&D editions and can often use 5e material

Why Play 5e Compatible Games?

Familiar Mechanics: If you know 5e, you already know most of the rules. Learning curve is minimal.

Content Compatibility: Use your existing 5e modules, monsters, and magic items with little or no conversion.

Variety Within Comfort: Experience different settings, genres, and playstyles while keeping the mechanics you enjoy.

Community Size: 5e is the most popular TTRPG, so finding players and resources is easy.
